Q2 MSQ Not checked
General Aptitude • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +1 / -0
Not attempted

The number 12375 is divisible by

Correct answer: A, B, C
Solution

(a, b, c)
• For a number to be divisible by 3 and 9, the sum of its digits should be divisible by 3 and 9
respectively. Here, 1 + 2 + 3 + 7 + 5 = 18, which is divisible 3 and 9 both. Hence, the number is
divisible by both 3 and 9.
• For a number to be divisible by 11, the difference between the sum of digits in the odd
positions and even positions must be a multiple of 11 or 0. Here, (5 + 3 + 1) – (7 + 2) = 0. Hence,
the number is divisible by 11.

General Aptitude • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Sum of all numbers n such that n! ends with 30 zeros.

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
124! ends with 28 zeros and 125! ends with 31 zeros.
Therefore, there is no integer n with n! ending in exactly 30 zeros.

Q9 MCQ Not checked
General Aptitude • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Read the passage carefully and choose the best answer to the following question from the four alternatives given below.
Among the natural resources which can be called upon in national plan for development, possibly
the most important is human labour; without productive labour force, including effective leadership
and intelligent middle management, no amount of foreign assistance or natural wealth can ensure
successful development and modernisation. One essential factor is usually overlooked or ignored.
The forgotten factor is the role of women. Development will be handicapped as long as women
remain second-class citizens, uneducated, without any voice in family or community decisions,
without legal or economic status, married when they are still practically children, and thenceforth
producing one baby after another, often only to see half of them die before they are of school age.
We can enhance development by improving ‘woman power’ by giving women opportunity to develop themselves.
The most important natural resource for national development is

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
The passage clearly states that among natural resources, human labour is the most important for national development.

Q18 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Match the items in List-I with the appropriate descriptions in List-II:

 
Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
• Minimax Algorithm → Strategy for decision-making in adversarial games
Used in two-player games (like chess, tic-tac-toe) to minimize possible loss.
• Priority Queue → Assigns priority to nodes using a queue
Priority Queue is a data structure that extracts the element with the highest priority first.
• Depth First → Search Explores as far as possible before backtracking.
DFS goes deep into a branch, then backtracks.

Q19 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

If the significance level (α) of a test is decreased, then generally:

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
The significance level (α) controls the probability of committing a Type I error. If α is decreased, the rejection region for the null hypothesis becomes smaller. This makes it harder to reject H0, even when it is false. Consequently, the probability of failing to reject H0 when it is false (Type II error, β) increases.
Since the power of a test is 1 – β, reducing α usually reduces the power.

Q24 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
S1 : PCA reduces dimensions by projecting data onto directions that maximize variance.
S2 : PCA requires class label information to perform dimensionality reduction.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
• Principal Component Analysis (PCA) finds orthogonal directions (principal components) that capture the maximum variance in the data. So, S1 is correct.
• PCA is unsupervised and does not require class labels; it only depends on the covariance
structure of features. So, S2 is incorrect.

Q31 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: A, C
Solution

(a, c)
• SVMs can handle non-linear data better than Naive Bayes because they use optimization
techniques to find the best separating hyperplane.
Non-linear separability can be addressed using Kernels, which Naive Bayes cannot exploit
effectively. So, option (a) is correct.
• Naive Bayes is a simple probabilistic classifier and requires very few or no hyperparameters
to tune. SVMs, on the other hand, need careful tuning of parameters like C, gamma, and
kernel type for good performance. So, option (b) is incorrect.
• SVMs use Kernel functions (e.g., RBF, polynomial) to map non-linear data into higherdimensional
space. This transformation allows the SVM to find a linear separating hyperplane
in the transformed space, effectively handling non-linear patterns. So, option (c) is correct.
• SVMs do require hyperparameter tuning when using non-linear Kernels. Parameters such as
C (regularization) and gamma (for RBF Kernel) significantly affect model performance. So,
option (d) is incorrect.

Q36 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following relational schema from a relational database:
Employee(Eid, Ename, SubDep, Department, Address)
{Eid, SubDep, Department} is primary key of relation Employee.
Which of the following query finds the Eid of employee who works in “Madeeasy” and “NextIAS” both department?

Correct answer: D, A, B, C
Solution

(d)
We need employee works in both departments, A tuple in a relation can have only one value for
department. So option (a) will return 0 tuples.
In option (b), it will return id who works in Madeeasy or NextIAS, but we need ‘And’ condition.
Same problem with option (c).

Q38 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: A, B, C
Solution

(a, b, c)
• Backward chaining is goal-driven. It begins with a query (goal) and recursively decomposes
it into subgoals. The search continues until the system finds supporting facts or fails. So,
option (a) is correct.
• Backward chaining is usually implemented with depth-first search (DFS) because DFS is
memory efficient and explores one branch of reasoning (subgoal expansion) at a time.
So, option (b) is correct.
• This is essentially the step-by-step description of backward chaining: start with the goal →
trace backwards through rules → verify against known facts. So, option (c) is correct.
• Forward chaining (data-driven reasoning), where a small number of facts can be expanded
into a large number of inferred conclusions. Backward chaining, in contrast, is focused on the
goal and only uses rules necessary to confirm or reject it. So, option (d) is incorrect.

Q43 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0
Not attempted

In the context of classification performance evaluation, consider the F1-score.
Which of the following statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: A, B, D
Solution

(a, b, d)
• F1-score is defined based on Precision and Recall, both of which are in [0,1]. Therefore, F1-
score also lies between 0 and 1, including 0 and 1. So, option (a) is correct.
• F1-score is the harmonic mean of Precision and Recall. So, option (b) is correct.
• The harmonic mean penalizes low values. If either Precision or Recall is very low, F1-score
will also be low. So it cannot be high if either value is very low. So, option (c) is incorrect.
• F1-score balances Precision and Recall. It is especially useful for imbalanced datasets, where
accuracy alone can be misleading. So, option (d) is correct.

Q47 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: A, B, C
Solution

(a, b, c)
• IDA* uses depth-first searches with increasing f-cost thresholds and does not store the full
frontier/closed set; space is linear in depth Ο(d) versus A*’s worst-case exponential memory
Ο(bd). So, option (a) is correct.
• IDA* restarts DFS from the root for each higher threshold, so the same nodes are regenerated
and expanded across iterations, causing multiple re-expansions. So, option (b) is correct.
• Formally, an admissible heuristic satisfies h(n) ≤ h * (n) for every node n, where h * (n) is the
true minimal cost to a goal; this property prevents overestimation and preserves A*’s
optimality. So, option (c) is correct.
• Greedy Best-First uses only h(n) (ignores path cost g(n)), so it can choose nodes that look
promising by heuristic but lead to suboptimal solutions; hence it is not guaranteed optimal.
So, option (d) is incorrect.

Q54 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
S1 : Slice operation selects a single value for one dimension and reduces the cube to a subcube.
S2 : Dice operation selects multiple values for multiple dimensions to create a subcube.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• Slice operation selects a single value along one dimension, effectively reducing the cube to a
subcube containing only that value. So, S1 is correct.
• Dice operation selects specific values across two or more dimensions, producing a subcube
containing all combinations of the selected values. So, S2 is correct.

Q64 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: A, B, C
Solution

(a, b, c)
• The t-distribution is symmetric and bell-shaped, like the normal distribution. So, option (a) is
correct.
• It has heavier tails to account for extra variability from estimating the population variance.
The tails get thinner as degrees of freedom increase. So, option (b) is correct.
• As sample size (or degrees of freedom) increases, the t-distribution converges to the standard
normal distribution. So, option (c) is correct.
• The mean of the t-distribution is 0, not 1. So, option (d) is incorrect.
